ead.archdesc:
======= RMM07606Alice Kleberg Reynolds papers7606Compiled by Human Sexuality Collection, RMCDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
June 20012B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
humsxThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:02:37 UTC.ENG12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Alice Kleberg Reynolds papers
Reynolds, Alice Kleberg.
7606
4.8 cubic feet. 4.8 cubic feet.
1979-2004. Subject files, ephemera. Professional and personal subject files pertaining chiefly to the Human Rights Campaign Fund, and its work in AIDS education, advocacy, queer politics, women's rights campaigns, and gay and lesbian social and political issues. Collection material in English 2797242
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Lisa Sasaki
Date completed:
1996
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, June 2001 Sara McDermott, January 2010
Date modified:
Jude Corina, July 2017
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Professional and personal subject files pertaining chiefly to the Human Rights Campaign Fund (HRCF), and its work in AIDS education, advocacy, queer politics, women's rights campaigns, and gay and lesbian social and political issues; includes queer ephemera, t-shirts, and buttons, as well as posters from HRCF and Texas political events, such as "Texas March to Stop the Violence, Stop the Hate" and a poster criticizing Senator Kay Bailey Hutchison. One large poster "Save the Amazons: Fight Breast Cancer," has "lesbian librarians" penciled at the bottom. Also, national posters from the 1993 March on Washington for Lesbian, Gay, and Bi Equal Rights and Liberation and other events. Includes publicity materials for San Antionio Black Tie dinners in 1995 and 1997, events that raised money for HRCF and local agencies.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Human Rights Campaign Fund
Boxes 1-2, 4-5, 10
Series II. Subject Files
Boxes 5-6
Series III. Ephemera
Boxes 3-4, 7-9, oversize folder
SEPARATED MATERIAL
Publications Removed From Collection 7606 - Cataloged As Serials In The Human Sexuality Collection:
10 Percent, San Francisco, CA. 2 issues.
1998 Capital Gains and Losses: A State by State Review of Gay, Lesbian, Bisexual, Transgender, and HIV/AIDS-Related Legislation in 1998. Washington, D.C.: NGLTF Policy Institute, 1998.
Advocate, Los Angeles, CA. 3 issues.
Aspen Gay Ski Week Program, Aspen, CO. 1 issue.
Atlanta Community Yellow Pages, Atlanta, GA. 1997; 1999-2000.
Bailey, Robert W. Out & Voting: The Gay, Lesbian and Bisexual Vote in Congressional House Elections, 1990-1996. Washington, D.C.: NGLTF Policy Institute, 1998.
Baltimore Gay Paper, Baltimore, MD. 1 issue.
Bay Area Reporter, San Francisco, CA. 2 issues.
Blueboy, New York, NY. 1 issue.
Boston Phoenix, Boston, MA. Supplement: "One in Ten: A queer students' guide to the best homo hangouts in Boston." Sept. 1996.
Damron Women's Traveller, 1998.
Ellas Dicen, San Antonio, TX. 3 issues.
ETC, Atlanta, GA. 3 issues.
Eurogames 2004, Munich.
Feminist Majority Report, Artlington, VA. 2 issues.
Gay Chicago Magazine, Chicago, IL. 1 issue.
Gayellow Pages: The National Edition: USA & Canada for Gay Women & Men, New York, NY, 1991.
H Inc., Denver, CO. 1 issue.
Houston Voice, Houston, TX. 4 issues.
HRC Quarterly, Washington, DC. 10 issues.
Khan, Surina. Calculated Compassion: How the Ex-Gay Movement Serves the Right's Attack on Democracy. Published by Political Research Associates, The Policy Institute of the National Gay and Lesbian Task Force, and Equal Partners in Faith, 1998.
Ladyslipper Catalog: Music by Women & More, Durham, NC. 1 issue.
Legacy, New York, NY. issues.
Lesbian Alliance of Metro Milwaukee, Milwaukee, WI. 6 issues.
Lesbian News, Torrance, CA. 1 issue.
National Queer Arts Festival 2003, San Francisco, CA.
National Women's Polictical Caucus, Women's Political Times, Washington, D.C. 2 issues.
Olivia Cruises & Resorts: Vacations for Women, Oakland, CA. 8 issues and several inserts.
Out, North Hollywood, CA. 6 issues.
OutFront Colorado, Denver, CO. 1 issue.
Outlook, Columbus, OH. 1 issue.
OutSmart, Houston, TX. 3 issues.
Passport, San Francisco, CA. 1 issue.
People for the American Way, Hostile Climate, Washington, DC. 1995, 1997.
Pride '03, San Francisco, CA. 1 issue.
Quest Magazine, Denver, CO. 1 issue.
San Antonio Community News,, San Antonio, TX. 1 issue.
San Antonio Current, San Antonio, TX. 3 issues.
San Antonio Marquise, San Antonio, TX. 12 issues.
San Antonio Unity Yellow Pages, San Antonio, TX. 1 issue.
SapphFire, Austin, TX. 2 issues.
Southern Voice, Atlanta, GA. 2 issues.
The Stateswoman, Madison, WI. 1 issue.
Storyletters, San Antonio, TX. 6 issues.
Texas Triangle, Austin, TX. 215 issues.
Texas Women's Political Times, Austin, TX. 1 issue.
This Week in Texas, Houston, TX. 1 issue.
Travel News from Remote Possibilities, Wailuku, HI. 1 issue.
Unity, Madison, WI. 1 issue.
La Voz de Esperanza, San Antonio, TX. 5 issues and Special 10-Year Booklet.
Wolfe Video, New Almaden, CA. 1 issue.
WomanSpace, San Antonio, TX. 33 issues.
Women's Community Cancer Project, Cambridge, MA. 2 issues.
Zebra'z, San Antonio, TX. 1 issue.
Cite As:
Alice Kleberg Reynolds papers, #7606. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
AIDS (Disease) -- Education. Lesbians -- Political activity. Gays -- Political activity. Human Rights Campaign Fund
